name: White Crackers
recipeIngredient: 2/3 Cup Milk, plus additional milk if needed
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
2. Place ungreased cookie sheets in the oven.
3. In a mixing bowl, combine flour, sugar, and half a teaspoon of salt.
4. Stir the dry ingredients until well blended.
5. Cut the butter into small pieces and add it to the dry mixture.
6. Using your fingertips or a pastry cutter, cut the butter into the dry mixture until it resembles fine crumbs.
7. Gradually stir in the milk, adding enough so that the dough pulls away from the sides of the bowl. If the dough is too dry, add a few drops more; it should be soft and pliable, not wet and sticky.
8. Divide the dough into two equal portions.
9. Lightly dust a surface with flour.
10. Roll each portion out to a square measuring approximately 13 x 13 inches and thinner than 1/8 inch.
11. Trim any uneven edges and transfer the dough onto the cookie sheets.
12. Use a sharp knife to score the dough into 2-inch squares, cutting almost through the dough.
13. Bake for 6-8 minutes, or until the edges are slightly golden.
14. Remove from the oven and use a large spatula to loosen the squares and flip them over.
15. Return the crackers to the oven and bake for another 5-6 minutes, or until the edges are well browned.
16. Remove from the oven and let the sheets cool on racks.
17. Break the crackers apart when cooled and store in an airtight container. These will keep for weeks and remain fresh.
Makes 5-dozen 2-inch crackers.
